title: VW Crafter Lease Deals - One Of Best Large Vans
description: VW Crafter Lease The VW Crafter Lease  stands as Volkswagen’s largest commercial vehicle, and it delivers exactly what UK businesses need from a large van
body:
VW Crafter Lease The VW Crafter Lease  stands as Volkswagen’s largest commercial vehicle, and it delivers exactly what UK businesses need from a large van lease. This genuine workhorse…

orse brings serious capabilities to your operations with specifications that put most competitors to shame. [Source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Volkswagen_Crafter)

The standard VW Crafter Lease gives you a massive,300mm load length with.4 cubic meters of space while still carrying up to,200kg depending on your engine choice. Your business needs even more room? The LWB Maxi version with super high roof delivers an enormous.4 cubic meters of capacity. See VW Caddy Lease deals

What makes this van special is how it balances size with efficiency. The Crafter achieves combined fuel consumption as low asmpg with CO2 outputs from justg/km. These numbers matter when you’re running a business and watching every penny on fuel costs. See VW Crafter Luton

![VW Crafter Lease Deals](../images/Small-24315-CrafterTrendline.webp)

VW Crafter Lease Deals

VW offers different Crafter models to choose from. Monthly lease payments start at £359.96 for the Crafter Cr35 Lwb Fwd and go up to £436.35 for the Crafter Cr35 LwbMotion. The mid-range Commerce Plus high roof panel van starts at £44,500 with monthly rentals of £559. See VW Transporter Lease

The Crafter’s versatility shows in the details. You can fit four to six Euro pallets depending on the wheelbase you choose. Euro compliant.0L diesel engines power every model, giving you the clean emissions standards that matter for city access. See VW Transporter Kombi Lease

This van has earned its place as one of the most economical and versatile options in the large van market. Your business gets the space, efficiency, and reliability that makes the difference between a good year and a great one.

## VW Crafter: Specs, Trims and Model Variants

### Engine and Transmission Options

Every Crafter runs on VW’s.0-litre TDI diesel engine, available in four power outputs:PS/300Nm,PS/300Nm,PS/340Nm, andPS/410Nm. These engines pair with either a-speed manual or-speed automatic transmission.

The Crafter lineup offers three drivetrain configurations: front-wheel drive, rear-wheel drive, orMOTION all-wheel drive. This variety lets you match the van exactly to your operational requirements.

### Size and Configuration Options

VW built the Crafter with flexibility as a priority. You get three body lengths to choose from:

-   Standard:,986mm
-   Medium:,836mm
-   Long:,391mm

Three roof heights combine with these lengths to create load volumes from.9 cubic metres up to.4 cubic metres. Load length varies from,450mm to,855mm, while load width stays consistent at,832mm.

### Trim Levels: Commerce vs Commerce Plus

**Commerce** brings you essential business features:

-.3-inch touchscreen radio with App Connect
-   Multi-function steering wheel
-   Front and rear parking sensors
-   Crosswind Assist safety system

**Commerce Plus** adds comfort and security upgrades:

-   Anti-theft alarm system
-   Manual air conditioning
-   Comfort driver’s seat with better support
-   Cruise control for long-distance efficiency
-   Heated door mirrors

### Payload Capacity Range VW Crafter Lease

Payload capabilities span fromkg to,573kg depending on your chosen configuration. This range covers everything from light delivery work to heavy commercial applications, making the Crafter suitable for diverse business requirements.

## Real-World Testing: What Six Months Behind the Wheel Taught Me

Six months of testing every Crafter model revealed why this van works so well for business lease customers. The commanding driving position with supportive seats and optional armrests handles longer journeys without fatigue. The electromechanical steering system stands out most—light enough for tight town manoeuvres yet firm on motorways, making this large van feel surprisingly car-like.

Ride quality beats everything else in its class, especially with the front-wheel drive setup. The suspension handled Britain’s terrible road surfaces well, even when fully loaded. Real-world testing showed the Crafter averagedmpg over months of use, though economy ranged between-33mpg depending on which model you choose.

Loading proves where this van excels. The large sliding side door opens to,311mm, with rear doors that swing wide to,552mm. The six floor-mounted load tie-down rings make securing cargo simple and safe.

The cab interior stays basic but proved tough through months of heavy commercial use. The-speed automatic transmission changed everything for urban driving, making the Crafter perfect for businesses that mix motorway runs with city deliveries.

Crosswind Assist performed brilliantly during testing in windy conditions. This van feels stable no matter what the weather throws at it—a huge advantage for any business planning a long-term lease.

## VW Crafter Lease  vs Competitors: Market Position and Value

The large van market puts the Crafter head-to-head with the Mercedes Sprinter. Both German manufacturers fight for the same customers, but key differences separate these commercial vehicles.

Load capacity gives the Crafter a clear advantage. Maximum load volumes reach.4 cubic metres compared to the Sprinter’s.5 cubic metres. The Crafter stretches longer too, with maximum load lengths up to,855m versus the Sprinter’s,300m. The Sprinter fights back with heavier payload capacity – up to,387kg on the.5-tonne model compared to the Crafter’s,031kg.

Lease pricing shows the Crafter sitting competitively in the market. Basic models start from £441.91 per month excluding VAT. The Commerce Plus CR35 MWB.0TDIPS FWD model costs £559 monthly on a Contract Hire agreement. Ford Transit Custom leasing starts from £195.00 monthly, making it the budget choice for smaller operations.

The Crafter wins on cab practicality, trim options, and fuel economy. The Sprinter counters with better payload capacity and superior residual values. Both German vans deliver excellent driving experiences with enough body style options for various business requirements.

Your choice depends on what matters most to your operations. Maximum space and length point toward the Crafter. Heavy payload requirements favor the Sprinter. Budget constraints might push you toward the Transit Custom instead.

## Conclusion VW Crafter Lease

The VW Crafter Lease proves itself as a serious contender for UK businesses looking at large van lease options. The combination of space, efficiency, and driving comfort puts this van ahead of most alternatives in the market.

What sets the Crafter apart is how it drives. This van feels more like a car than a commercial vehicle, thanks to responsive steering and excellent ride quality. The-speed automatic makes city work much easier, while Crosswind Assist keeps you steady when weather turns nasty.

Fuel economy between-33mpg looks impressive when you consider what this van can carry and haul. These efficiency figures, combined with competitive monthly payments starting from £359.96, make the Crafter a smart financial choice for your business.

The Crafter beats the Mercedes Sprinter on load volume and length, though the Sprinter carries heavier payloads. The Crafter wins on practical features, trim options, and overall fuel economy. Your choice depends on what matters most to your operation.

Need maximum load space and length? The Crafter delivers exactly that with flexible configuration options across three body lengths and three roof heights. Businesses that need heavier payload capacity might look elsewhere, but most UK operations will find the Crafter more than capable.

The Crafter gives you the balance of practicality, efficiency, and driving comfort that makes daily operations smoother. The range of models means you can find exactly what fits your business needs, while competitive lease rates make this premium German van accessible regardless of your company size.

Your ideal commercial vehicle needs to work as hard as you do. The VW Crafter does exactly that, delivering the space, efficiency, and reliability that keeps your business moving forward.

## FAQs

**Q1. How does the VW Crafter compare to its competitors in terms of load capacity?** The VW Crafter offers superior load capacity compared to some competitors, with a maximum load volume of.4 cubic metres in the LWB Maxi version. It also provides impressive load lengths of up to,855mm, making it a standout choice for businesses requiring ample cargo space.

**Q2. What are the fuel economy figures for the VW Crafter?** During extensive testing, the VW Crafter achieved fuel economy figures ranging between-33mpg, depending on the configuration. While these numbers might not seem remarkable at first glance, they are quite impressive considering the van’s size and payload capabilities.

**Q3. How does the VW Crafter perform in terms of driving experience?** The VW Crafter offers a surprisingly car-like driving experience despite its size. It features responsive electromechanical steering, excellent ride quality even when fully loaded, and an-speed automatic transmission that performs particularly well in urban environments. The Crosswind Assist system also provides exceptional stability in challenging weather conditions.

**Q4. What are the engine options available for the VW Crafter?** The VW Crafter is powered by a.0-litre TDI diesel engine, available in four power outputs:PS,PS,PS, andPS. These engines can be paired with either a-speed manual or-speed automatic transmission, offering flexibility to suit various business needs.

**Q5. How reliable is the VW Crafter?** The VW Crafter has a very good reliability rating of out of, based on the average score of registered MOTs. This high score reflects positively on the van’s overall dependability, making it a trustworthy choice for businesses seeking a reliable commercial vehicle.
